preparing.title: Preparing the showcase
preparing.description: RxFHIR is switching into showcase mode and loading a stable demo snapshot.

completed.title: Showcase completed
completed.description: The main product highlights have been presented. You can close the tour or replay it.

reducedMotionHint: Reduced motion is enabled, so the spotlight and coach stay more static and autoplay uses shorter pauses.

appShell.title: App Shell
appShell.summary: The showcase begins with the overall layout so viewers can understand the product structure first.
appShell.feature: The sidebar, top utility controls, and bottom status bar form a consistent app shell across Creator, Consumer, Settings, and About.
appShell.problem: Multi-page tools are hard to present when users cannot quickly build a spatial model of the product.
appShell.role: The app shell is the shared frame for every workflow and keeps navigation stable throughout the demo.

creatorWorkflow.title: Creator Step-by-Step Workflow
creatorWorkflow.summary: This area demonstrates how RxFHIR builds each resource in a guided sequence.
creatorWorkflow.feature: The stepper breaks Organization, Patient, Practitioner, and other resources into a clear sequence with completion status and summaries.
creatorWorkflow.problem: FHIR documents are complex, and a single large form would make the structure much harder to teach and operate.
creatorWorkflow.role: Creator is the primary authoring experience that takes users from individual resources to a final document bundle.

fillMock.title: Fill Mock
fillMock.summary: Mock data can populate realistic scenarios quickly for teaching, testing, and live demos.
fillMock.feature: The mock system is scenario-based rather than random field stuffing, and it stays consistent across resources and locales.
fillMock.problem: Typing every clinical value by hand slows down demonstrations and makes repeatable teaching harder.
fillMock.role: Fill Mock is the accelerator that moves Creator into a realistic working state in seconds.

jsonPreview.title: Resource JSON Preview
jsonPreview.summary: The right panel lets viewers inspect the FHIR resource structure as the form evolves.
jsonPreview.feature: The JSON panel supports font size control, collapse behavior, and latest-resource focus, making the data model visible and teachable.
jsonPreview.problem: A form alone hides the FHIR structure and makes it difficult to connect UI fields to resource content.
jsonPreview.role: This panel bridges the UI and the actual FHIR resource representation.

requestInspector.title: FHIR Request Inspector
requestInspector.summary: This panel shows how the app actually talks to the FHIR server.
requestInspector.feature: The inspector presents request flow, HTTP methods, URLs, and request/response details in a compact Postman-like view.
requestInspector.problem: Without visible HTTP traffic, it is hard to explain what the app is really doing with the server.
requestInspector.role: It turns front-end actions into observable FHIR API interactions.

bundleAssembly.title: Composition and Document Bundle
bundleAssembly.summary: The final step assembles individual resources into a complete electronic prescription document.
bundleAssembly.feature: RxFHIR creates Composition and then assembles a document Bundle while keeping identifiers and submission results visible.
bundleAssembly.problem: A document is more than a list of resources; it needs document-level meaning and relationships.
bundleAssembly.role: This is the final packaging step that converts the authoring workflow into a full FHIR document.

consumerSearch.title: Consumer Search Modes
consumerSearch.summary: Consumer supports multiple search modes for different usage scenarios.
consumerSearch.feature: Basic, date-based, and complex search are organized in a single panel, with mock fill and prefill support for fast lookup.
consumerSearch.problem: Real searches rarely rely on one single condition, so users need more than one search path.
consumerSearch.role: Consumer is the retrieval side of the system and brings submitted bundles back into view.

consumerQuickStart.title: Search Helpers
consumerQuickStart.summary: Recent submissions and saved searches help users return to meaningful queries quickly.
consumerQuickStart.feature: The quick-start pane gathers recent complete bundles, favorite searches, and recent searches into one recovery surface.
consumerQuickStart.problem: Rebuilding the same queries repeatedly slows down demos and everyday validation work.
consumerQuickStart.role: This area shortens the path from previous work to a new search.

consumerResults.title: Search Results
consumerResults.summary: Bundle search results are summarized in a card-based list for fast scanning.
consumerResults.feature: Each result highlights patient, date, organization, and medication clues so users can identify the right prescription quickly.
consumerResults.problem: Raw results or sparse labels make it hard to distinguish one prescription from another.
consumerResults.role: The result list connects search input with the detail viewer.

consumerStructuredDetail.title: Structured Detail View
consumerStructuredDetail.summary: The detail panel turns a technical bundle into a readable clinical summary.
consumerStructuredDetail.feature: Patient, practitioner, organization, condition, and medication data are grouped into readable sections instead of raw JSON only.
consumerStructuredDetail.problem: A raw FHIR bundle is complete but not ideal for teaching or quick review.
consumerStructuredDetail.role: Structured detail is the main reading mode for Consumer.

consumerRawJson.title: Raw JSON View
consumerRawJson.summary: The same prescription can also be inspected as raw bundle JSON.
consumerRawJson.feature: Structured and JSON views can be switched instantly, supporting both human-readable demos and technical validation.
consumerRawJson.problem: A single display mode cannot satisfy both non-technical and technical audiences.
consumerRawJson.role: Raw JSON provides a direct view into the source bundle structure.

settingsServer.title: FHIR Server Settings
settingsServer.summary: Settings allow the app to switch between target FHIR environments.
settingsServer.feature: Users can edit the server URL, apply presets, and persist the preferred environment.
settingsServer.problem: Teaching, testing, and showcasing often require switching servers quickly.
settingsServer.role: This is the environment control center for the entire app.

settingsStatus.title: Health Check and Status
settingsStatus.summary: The app continuously exposes environment status for better observability.
settingsStatus.feature: Health check results show online/offline state, server name, and FHIR version in one place.
settingsStatus.problem: Without clear environment status, it is difficult to tell whether an issue comes from data, connectivity, or the server itself.
settingsStatus.role: This area provides system observability and confidence when working across environments.

appControls.title: Theme and Language Controls
appControls.summary: RxFHIR supports quick visual and language switching for demos and real usage.
appControls.feature: The app can switch between Chinese and English as well as light, dark, and system themes while keeping the experience consistent.
appControls.problem: A teaching-oriented app needs to adapt quickly to different audiences and display conditions.
appControls.role: These controls demonstrate product flexibility beyond the core FHIR workflow.

statusBar.title: Status Bar
statusBar.summary: The bottom status bar keeps critical environment information visible at all times.
statusBar.feature: It surfaces server health, FHIR version, and the current module in a compact persistent layer.
statusBar.problem: Users can lose context quickly in multi-page tools if system status is hidden.
statusBar.role: The status bar is the persistent context layer that supports every page in the app.
